messages.content: How to catch scroll event before it happens. I mean I can add listener to scroll event but it already scrolls some distance and I want to catch it before it happens because I have my custom scrolling animation and I don't want any scrolling to happen. I can not listen to wheel event because then I am missing keyboard or other APIs scrolling events.
are you sure, even thought I got prevent default, my function scrolls a bit before starting animation
I mean also wheel does not include scrolling with keyboard

well how about VR gesture stuff. As you can see there are tons of APIs to consider, isn't there a solution to prevent scrolling before it happens ? Not even when using stuff like astro, preact, tailwind, typescript ?
what about using astro and preact and tailwind etc. ?
I mean is there only these 4 things that can scroll ?

No, there are many different input devices and APIs that can trigger scrolling events in a web browser. The four input devices and APIs I mentioned earlier (mouse wheel, touchpad, arrow keys, and VR gestures) are just some of the most common ones.

Other input devices and APIs that can trigger scrolling events include:

- Scrollbars
- Scroll buttons on mice
- Trackballs
- Joysticks
- Gamepads
- Touchscreens
- Scroll events triggered by JavaScript code

To handle scrolling events from these input devices and APIs, you may need to use different event listeners and APIs than the ones I mentioned earlier. However, the basic approach of using event listeners to capture scrolling events and prevent default scrolling behavior should still apply.

okay what about, when the scroll event is captured, we reverse its effects immediately before playing animation ? I am not sure if this will look smooth or glitchy. Also reversing the scroll event must be in the same frame, immediately so that animation play from the beginning to end.

yes but the problem is user is already scrolling half way down the animation. Then can I set the maximum scroll to 1 etc. So that no matter what wheel sensitivity user has they will maximum scroll one unit, which is okay. I just don't want user to already scroll down half the animation.
yes but similarly to previous responses, this requires to consider many input devices other then mouse, like keyboard, VR, touchpad, touchscreen etc.
okay what about css, scroll start and scroll end stuff ?
